paragraphs.body: Title: The Magnificent Beauty of the Cathedral of Learning Main Point: Cover the similarities and differences between the Cathedral of Learning and TV Tower in my hometown XuZhou. Describe the features and beauties outside of these two buildings. Including the details of decoration scenes inside of the buildings, and sharing the feelings and emotions when I truly see them.

Exposition: Before I came to Pittsburgh, I thought it was a very friendly and quiet and civilized city. I have a longing for Pittsburgh and I think it is a clean and nice city. I would like to see students helping each other. The name 'Cathedral of Education' evokes images of a grand, stately, perhaps gothic, structure of historic importance. I am looking forward to seeing the ornate interiors, the vast halls, and the rich connotations of culture and education.

Conflict: After visiting the Cathedral of Learning, I realized that it is indeed a magnificent building. One of the things that surprised me was its unique blend of Gothic, Renaissance and modern architectural styles. The interior is equally stunning, with impressively staggered classrooms and study areas. The cultural diversity and academic vigor of the place exceeded my initial expectations. Often the thought that I will be studying here for four years overwhelms me. Especially compared to the TV tower in my hometown Xuzhou, I think they are completely different buildings. . Rising action: I compared the Cathedral of Learning with the TV tower in Xuzhou.

I found that the two differ in their historical importance and architectural significance, but also in their use of language, cultural symbols and overall atmosphere. In particular, there is a huge difference in the style of decoration.

Climax：It made me realize that while grandeur and significance are common features of these places, the specific language, cultural symbols, and historical narratives vary from place to place. The overall building heights are almost identical, but there are significant differences in the layout.

Conclusion: Cross-culturally broadened my understanding of cultural diversity as each building has its own take on a unique narrative and historical context. These have helped me to deepen my understanding and appreciation of the richness and diversity
